```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
set.seed(sample.int(.Machine$integer.max, 1))

## Parametros
xbar <- round(runif(1, 3, 9), 2)
s    <- round(runif(1, 0.5, 3), 2)
n    <- sample(c(10, 15, 20, 25), 1)

t_crit <- round(qt(0.975, n - 1), 4)

## Respostas: margem de erro do IC t
correct <- round(qt(0.975, n - 1) * s / sqrt(n), 4)
w1 <- round(1.96 * s / sqrt(n), 4)
w2 <- round(qt(0.975, n - 1) * s / n, 4)
w3 <- round(qt(0.975, n) * s / sqrt(n), 4)
w4 <- round(qt(0.975, n - 1) * s * sqrt(n), 4)

all_vals <- c(correct, w1, w2, w3, w4)
# Garante cinco alternativas numericamente distintas após a parametrização.
integer_choices <- all(abs(all_vals - round(all_vals)) < sqrt(.Machine$double.eps))
choice_step <- if (integer_choices) 1 else max(1e-4, max(abs(all_vals), na.rm = TRUE) * 0.01)
while (anyDuplicated(all_vals)) {
  duplicate_position <- which(duplicated(all_vals))[1]
  all_vals[duplicate_position] <- max(all_vals, na.rm = TRUE) + choice_step
}
choices <- sample(all_vals)
sol_pos <- which(choices == correct)
sol_str <- paste(as.integer(seq_along(choices) == sol_pos), collapse = "")
```

Question
========
Para $X_i \overset{\text{iid}}{\sim} N(\mu,\sigma^2)$ com $\sigma^2$ desconhecido, o IC 95% para $\mu$ usa o pivo $T = (\bar{X}-\mu)/(S/\sqrt{n}) \sim t_{n-1}$. Com $\bar{X} = `r xbar`$, $S = `r s`$ e $n = `r n`$, o IC e $\bar{X} \pm t_{`r n-1`,\,0{,}975} \cdot S/\sqrt{n}$. Usando $t_{`r n-1`,\,0{,}975} = `r t_crit`$, a margem de erro e:

Answerlist
----------
* `r choices[1]`
* `r choices[2]`
* `r choices[3]`
* `r choices[4]`
* `r choices[5]`

Solution
========
A margem de erro e $t_{`r n-1`,\,0{,}975} \times S/\sqrt{n} = `r t_crit` \times `r s` / \sqrt{`r n`} = `r correct`$.

Erros comuns: usar $z_{0{,}975}=1{,}96$ (ignorar $\sigma^2$ desconhecido); dividir por $n$ em vez de $\sqrt{n}$; usar $t_{n,\,0{,}975}$ com $n$ graus (em vez de $n-1$); multiplicar por $\sqrt{n}$.

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap5S2Q2_IC_t_mu
extype: schoice
exsolution: `r sol_str`
exshuffle: FALSE
